_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-30 11:22:13Z)**_

This is a pure CSS/layout change in a mockup file (`docs/vercel_mockup/src/MOCKUP.jsx`). The diff:

1. Changes the grid from `grid-cols-2` to `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2` (responsive stacking).
2. Adds `break-words` / `break-all` Tailwind classes so long agency names / ID numbers wrap instead of overflow.

Data-flow analysis:
- The bound values (`b.agency`, `b.designation`, `b.idType`, `b.idNumber`) were already rendered in the previous version via standard JSX `{...}` interpolation, which is auto-escaped by React. No new bindings introduced.
- No new sinks (no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no URL/path/shell/SQL/eval/log usage).
- No entry points, no auth/authz logic, no network calls, no state changes.
- The file is the static Vercel mockup (per project memory note about `docs/vercel_mockup`), not production app code.

No source→sink path exists; the change is presentational only.

No security findings. The change only adjusts Tailwind grid classes (`grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2`) and adds `break-words`/`break-all` wrapping to existing JSX text bindings in a mockup file — no new sinks, no new data exposure, no auth/authz logic.
